title: Non-record: AETHER block-sparse attention with Lyapunov-stable governor (12L)
body:
## Summary

Integrates AETHER (Adaptive Event-driven Threshold Hybrid Entangled Rendering) block-sparse attention into the SOTA baseline, enabling a **12-layer** architecture within the same 10-minute training budget on 8xH100.

## What Changed

- **Block-sparse SDPA**: Partition KV into 64-token blocks, compute Cauchy-Schwarz upper bounds on attention scores, prune low-relevance blocks via top-k selection. Falls back to dense SDPA for short sequences.

- **Geometric Governor**: Lyapunov-stable PD controller adaptively adjusts sparsity level during training. Proven contraction: |e_{t+1}| <= |e_t| (no oscillation or divergence).

- **Chebyshev GC Guard**: Bounds false reclamation at <= n/k^2 blocks (25%% at k=2).

- **Architecture**: 12 layers (up from 10), MLP 2.5x (down from 3.0x), 4%% magnitude pruning (up from 3%%).

- **All SOTA techniques preserved**: int5/int6 QAT, BigramHash(10240), SmearGate, SWA, Muon WD=0.04, sliding window eval.

## Mathematical Safety (Lean 4 Verified)

- PruneSafety.lean: Cauchy-Schwarz pruning soundness + tightness witness

- Governor.lean: Lyapunov descent theorem for PD controller

- ChebyshevGC.lean: Finite-sample gc_25_percent_bound

## Files

- records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-22_AETHER_SparseSDP_Governor/train_gpt.py (1438 lines)

- records/.../docs/ -- Full AETHER formulation, technical spec, and Lean 4 proofs

## Status

Pending 3-seed H100 validation runs to confirm val_bpb. Code is submission-ready.


Non-record experimental submission
